summary: DHH announces that Dave Thomas and Andy Hunt of the Pragmatic Programmers are sponsoring the Rails visual identity community fundraiser by matching community contributions. With their pledge to double donations, the $1,000 goal was nearly reached instantly. DHH expresses gratitude and reciprocates by promoting the Pragmatic Starter Kit and the upcoming 2nd edition of Programming Ruby. The fundraiser ultimately hit its goal in just 7 hours.
key_points: The Pragmatic Programmers (Dave Thomas and Andy Hunt) pledged to match community donations for the Rails visual identity project
The $1,000 fundraising goal was reached in only 7 hours, demonstrating early Rails community enthusiasm
DHH credits the first edition of Programming Ruby as the book that got him into Ruby
The post reflects the scrappy, community-funded origins of Ruby on Rails before it became a major framework
money_quotes: What an incredibly feat and testimony to the power of open source communities.
I'm really moved that Dave and Andy are taken notice of Ruby on Rails in such a significant way as to pledge $500 in its support.
It was the first edition that got me into Ruby in the first place and its still the book I use most in my daily work.
You need this book if you're thinking about or already doing Ruby. Whether or not it's on Rails.
The last $10 was donated. The goal of $1,000 has been reached. It took 7 hours.
key_insight: The Rails visual identity fundraiser reaching $1,000 in seven hours — with backing from the Pragmatic Programmers — captures the grassroots community energy that fueled Ruby on Rails in its earliest days.
